version: v1 (FALSIFIED — see falsificationNote; superseded by v2)
interpretation: FALSIFIED PROBE — do not read 0.5 as 'mixed positive evidence'. This v1 result is a known null: the probe conflated answer-token removal with reasoning perturbation (the _drop_last_sentence perturb deletes the 'Answer:' line the decider reads), and the binary yes/no decider is meaningless for the 'possibly' probe. The uniform 0.5 across load-bearing/hedged/post-hoc categories is a structural artifact, not a measurement. Retained on record as the falsified first version; v2 (build_mlx_decide_gold + reasoning-only perturbs) is the discriminating probe.
falsificationNote: Two compounding design flaws guarantee every probe lands at ~0.5 regardless of CoT category: (1) the decider forces argmax(yes,no) over a question whose gold answer may be 'possibly'; (2) the _drop_last_sentence perturb removes the answer token the decider reads, so it tests 'does deleting the answer change the answer' (trivially yes, trivially uniform). The probe measured perturbation strength, not faithfulness. Diagnosed post-run; v2 fixes both.
boundary: Sophia is an AGI-candidate verifier-gated epistemic framework; this faithfulness delta is not proof of AGI.
